meta.title: carl — Roadmap
meta.blurb: A living plan for the game. We edit roadmap.json together: flip statuses, add items as we learn from playing. Keep it honest — if something's half-done or unproven, say so.
meta.vision: A classic fixed-path tower-defense game: enemies advance along set paths in waves, and you place & upgrade towers along them to stop them before they reach the goal. Built in Godot 4 (pivoted from an earlier 3D action prototype — same engine, rebuilt gameplay).
meta.note: Pre-prototype for the TD direction. Placeholder primitives only; reuse engine + tooling, rebuild the game.

items.title: Multi-resolution / HiDPI display support
items.note: 3D scene fills any viewport naturally; work is UI anchoring + two project.godot settings. Chosen approach: expand (bigger screens see more map — right for a desktop strategy game, not letterboxed). No changes to camera or game logic unless extreme ultrawide aspect ratios become a balance issue. [Shipped: stretch=canvas_items/expand + allow_hidpi=true in project.godot; HUD top-right cluster (Maps/Demo/Start-wave) and build-type row re-anchored in td_hud.gd so the layout holds at any window size, verified headed at 800x600 and 1920x1080.]

children.title: Add display/window/dpi/allow_hidpi=true to project.godot so the game renders at native Retina resolution on HiDPI Macs instead of appearing zoomed-out
Add display/window/stretch/mode=canvas_items + display/window/stretch/aspect=expand so UI Control nodes scale with the window while the 3D viewport fills the screen

Audit all UI scenes: replace hardcoded pixel positions with anchor-based layouts (anchor presets: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right, full-rect) so the HUD stays correct at any window size
Set a sensible base window size in project.godot (e.g. 1280x720) as the reference resolution that UI anchors are authored against

items.title: Map verticality — multi-height terrain, elevated tower spots, paths that duck behind cover (LOS + spherical range groundwork is in)
items.note: LOS + spherical range checks are already in place, so elevation is a layer on top of existing groundwork.

children.title: Height tiers: define 2-3 discrete elevation levels in map data; towers placed on high ground get a range bonus and can see over low cover
Branching paths: enemy routes fork at junctions; each branch is a valid full path to the goal so enemies randomly pick at each fork — creates unpredictable spread across the map

Tower targeting priority: when a tower has enemies on multiple branches in range, prefer the one furthest along its path (existing logic); expose a per-tower priority toggle (furthest / nearest / lowest HP) in the HUD
Cover: elevated terrain segments block LOS for towers on equal or lower ground, but not for towers above — creates meaningful placement decisions
Map editor support: mark tiles as elevated or cover in the map JSON; td_game reads this when building the nav mesh and range-check geometry
Visual treatment: ramp meshes between height tiers; elevated tiles get a slightly lighter material so the height reads clearly in the isometric view
tower that buffs attack speed for friendly towers within range
tower that heals other towers within range
tower that casts a spell on enemies and makes them walk backwards

items.title: Meta-progression between runs (unlocks, persistent upgrades)
items.note: Gives players a reason to replay and a sense of long-term growth beyond a single run.

children.title: Persistent currency: earn a small amount of hard currency per run based on waves survived and enemies killed; carries over on loss
Unlock tree: spend hard currency to permanently unlock new tower types, enemy info cards, or starting bonuses (extra gold, an extra life) for future runs
Tower skins: cosmetic variants per tower type unlocked through the tree or achievements — no stat changes, pure expression
Per-run stats screen: waves survived, total damage dealt per tower type, most valuable tower, gold efficiency — shown on game-over and victory
Achievement badges: milestone unlocks (first win, survive wave 10 with no leaks, kill 500 enemies with beam towers, etc.) displayed on a profile screen
Save format: a small JSON file in user:// tracking currency, unlocks, and lifetime stats; designed so adding new unlock nodes never breaks old saves

items.title: map improvements
items.note: we should make the map prettier

children.title: smooth pathing
add texture to map tiles
improve view of the "base" entity (enemy goal)
when a tower is destroyed, it's zone goes into a 5s timeout
improve wall assets and add more detail

items.title: Navigation: map picker, in-game escape, pause, game-over/victory screens
items.note: Map picker on load is done. In-game escape back to the map picker is next. Pause screen and post-game screens (stats, retry, back to menu) still to do.

children.title: Escape / back button from in-game returns to the map picker (next up)
Pause screen (Esc mid-wave): resume, restart, back to map picker
Game-over and victory screens with stats (waves survived, kills, time) and retry / menu buttons

items.title: More combat juice — hit particles, screen shake, death effects, SFX

children.title: change enemy firing style
change enemy styles

items.title: Roadmap 2.0
items.note: A major rewrite of the Roadmap tool, shifting to a kanban layout, expanded data model, and more of a custom look and feel.

children.title: Redesign "Type" and "Status" properties
Remove starring / favoriting from tickets entirely
Remove edit button from cards; child tasks hidden by default and revealed by clicking the card

Change to a Kanban layout for Stories/Bugs and their child tasks. Status columns are collapsible — click the header to toggle, arrow rotates to indicate state; remaining columns expand to fill full width when any column is collapsed.
Lane collapse state persisted in localStorage and restored on reload

items.title: Unified per-enemy damage number (Borderlands-style accumulator)
items.note: Shipped 2026-06-09. One pinned DamageNumber per enemy counts up across all concurrent damage sources (beam, bullet, bomb). Follows the enemy as it moves. Lingers 0.25s after the last hit, then releases to drift and fade. All per-tower beam accumulator machinery removed — enemy owns the display entirely.

children.title: Move the accumulator onto TDEnemy: a running float (_dmg_accum) and a linger timer (_dmg_linger). All damage paths (take_damage, take_damage_silent) add to _dmg_accum and reset the timer.

TDEnemy owns the single pinned DamageNumber node for its lifetime. It acquires one on first hit (if none active), updates its text each frame, and releases it to drift+fade once the linger timer expires with no new hits.
Remove the per-tower _beam_number / _beam_dmg_accum machinery from TDTower — the tower calls take_damage_silent as before but the enemy handles all display.
Linger window: 0.25s after the last hit before the number is released. Any new damage resets the linger timer and keeps the number pinned and counting.
The pinned number tracks the enemy's world position each frame (enemy moves, number follows). On release it stays at the release position and drifts upward from there.
Bomb AoE hits multiple enemies simultaneously — each enemy's own accumulator handles it independently, so simultaneous multi-enemy hits each show their own correct total.
Remove the old _pending_dmg accumulator (was per-enemy but only guarded against sub-1 beam flicker, not multi-source overlap).

items.title: Damage types + enemy health types system (replaces frost tower)
items.note: Shipped 2026-06-09. Frost tower removed. DamageType enum (PHYSICAL/FIRE/FROST/POISON/SHOCK) + modifier matrix in TDTower. Damage branch chosen at level 2 via HUD picker. Enemy health layered (shield→armor→flesh, sequential). Health bar shows colored segments. Frost type retains slow. All 48 tests passing.

children.title: REMOVE frost tower type from TDTower.TYPES; update HUD, tests, and completed roadmap entry
Add damage type enum: PHYSICAL, FIRE, FROST, POISON, SHOCK — stored per tower, defaults PHYSICAL at build
Upgrade branch at level 2: player picks a damage type; choice is permanent and reflected in level 2+3 stat tiers in TYPES table
Frost damage type retains the slow effect (reduced enemy speed for a duration) in addition to its modifier matrix

Add health type system to TDEnemy: every enemy has shield_hp, armor_hp, flesh_hp (any can be 0). Damage flows strictly in order: shield fully depleted first, then armor fully depleted, then flesh. No simultaneous draining.

Damage modifier matrix (applied per pool as damage flows through): fire: flesh 1.0, armor 0.75, shield 1.5 | frost: flesh 1.0, armor 1.25, shield 0.75 | poison: flesh 1.5, armor 1.25, shield 0.5 | shock: flesh 1.0, armor 0.5, shield 2.0 | physical: all 1.0
Health bar visual: shield segment shown in blue above the existing green/red bar; armor shown in yellow; flesh stays green. Segments sized proportionally to their max HP
Update existing enemy types with health type compositions: Grunt = flesh-only, Healer = flesh+armor mix, Gunner = flesh+shield mix — exact splits TBD from playtesting
Wire damage type into take_damage() so modifiers apply automatically; beam DPS path included
Add GUT tests: modifier matrix correctness, shield-first ordering, armor/flesh proportional drain, frost slow still applies on frost-typed hit
Update VISUAL_CHECKLIST.md: colored health bar segments, upgrade branch UI, frost-typed tower still slows

items.title: Debug/perf overlay (bottom-left, debug builds only)
items.note: for diagnosing combat lag ahead of a bug-fix-or-rewrite decision

children.title: FPS + CPU/physics frame time (ms), object/node/orphan counts, draw calls + primitives, live enemy/projectile counts
Visible only in debug builds (OS.is_debug_build); auto-hidden in release
Projectiles/bombs join a td_projectile group for the live count

items.title: Test suite (GUT) + visual checklist
items.note: a pre-PR gate; caught a real current_scene-null crash on first run

children.title: Vendored GUT addon; run_tests.sh runs it headlessly and exits non-zero on failure
21 coded tests: economy (build/upgrade/sell), targeting + line-of-sight, AoE falloff, frost slow, beam DPS, lead prediction, wave/lives flow
test/VISUAL_CHECKLIST.md: prose checks for visual/feel items, run agentically (launch + screenshot)
Hardened DamageNumber.popup and the bomb blast against a null current_scene

items.title: Combat feedback: health bars, damage numbers, click-off dismiss

children.title: Enemy health bars (green over red): appear on damage/heal, hold 2s, fade 0.5s; any change resets the timer; billboard to camera. Reusable component for anything with a Health node.
Floating damage numbers (white, rounded, drift up + fade); beam's fractional DPS accumulates so it pops whole numbers
Dismiss the tower upgrade panel by clicking empty space or pressing Esc; clicking another tower switches

items.title: Bomb tower (4th type) + projectile speed
items.note: predictive AoE mortar that can whiff on turns

children.title: Lobs a bomb in an arc to a FIXED ground point = target pos + velocity × flight-time (lead prediction)
Landing point locks at fire time, so enemies that turn off their line get missed — intended weakness
AoE on landing with linear falloff (full at center → 40% at the edge → nothing outside); expanding blast visual
Projectile speed is now a per-tier stat on all towers and rises with upgrades; cannon/frost shots travel faster, and bomb speed tightens the lead
Data-driven via TYPES (bomb flag + aoe); HUD button/panel handle it automatically

items.title: Beam tower (3rd type)
items.note: continuous-DPS contrast to cannon burst / frost slow

children.title: Fires a solid beam at one in-range, in-LOS target; applies low damage continuously (DPS)
Instant retarget to the next valid enemy; single-target
Data-driven via TYPES (beam flag + dps tiers); HUD build button + upgrade panel pick it up automatically

items.title: Spherical range + line-of-sight + range preview
items.note: first step toward maps with verticality

children.title: Towers target within a true 3D (spherical) radius
Line-of-sight: a tower only fires at enemies it can see — raycast from the muzzle vs. blocker geometry (layer 4)
Demo obstacle (a raised wall) added to the map to show LOS blocking in action
Translucent range sphere/dome preview: shown while hovering a free slot (for the chosen type) and on a selected tower
Reusable hover-preview sphere in the controller; per-tower sphere shown on select

items.title: First playable TD prototype (core loop)
items.note: 3D iso, fixed path, fixed buildable slots — the full minimal loop is playable

children.title: L-shaped path via Path3D → waypoints; ground, slots, red goal; lit iso scene (reused)
Enemies walk the path (reuse health.gd); leaking costs a life, kills pay currency
Click a green slot to build a tower (costs currency); towers auto-target the leader & fire
Homing projectile so shots connect with moving creeps
Waves (5), economy (kills + start bank), 20 lives, win/lose, R to restart
HUD: lives / currency / wave + Start-wave button + messages; new td_main.tscn is the main scene

items.title: 2nd tower type + tiered upgrades + sell
items.note: the variety that makes it a game, not a tech demo. NOTE: frost tower is being removed and replaced by the damage type upgrade system.

children.title: Frost tower: lower damage, slows enemies on hit (blue tint + slow-factor/duration); enemies have a slow effect — REMOVED, slow mechanic folded into frost damage type upgrade
Two build-type buttons (Cannon / Frost) with costs; pick one, then click a slot
Tiered upgrades (Lv 1→3) via a click-to-select tower panel; stats + rising cost, capped at max
Sell a placed tower for 50% of total spent; frees the slot
Data-driven tower stats (TYPES table) so balancing/new tiers is a one-place edit

items.title: Camera pan, orbit, and zoom controls
items.note: Left-drag pans; right-drag orbits azimuth/elevation; scroll wheel zooms. Implemented in td_camera.gd. Perspective projection (45° FOV). Zoom step fine-grained (2.5% per scroll notch) for precise levels.

items.title: Enemy type variety: Grunt / Healer / Gunner
items.note: Data-driven TDEnemy.TYPES table (mirrors the tower TYPES). Spawner mixes types by wave — Healers from wave 2, Gunners from wave 3.

children.title: Explosive Grunt (main creep): orange blast on reaching the goal; on death a small AoE damages nearby towers
Healer: green heal-pulse on an interval that restores HP to nearby living allies (skips itself), with an expanding aura visual
Gunner: carries a turret that shoots the nearest tower in range (yellow tracer), on a per-shot cooldown
Towers are now destructible — Health + take_damage + white hit-flash; at 0 HP they're destroyed, free their slot, and close the panel if selected
11 new GUT tests cover type stats, heal aura range/self-skip, gunner range, explosive death blast, and tower destruction/slot cleanup

items.title: Performance pass
items.note: Used inline PerfTimer (Time.get_ticks_usec) instrumentation after the in-editor profiler couldn't show function trees. Steady 60fps confirmed post-fixes.

children.title: Health-bar billboard: ~16ms → ~3ms. Was doing get_camera_3d() + look_at() per bar each frame; now uses one shared facing basis refreshed only when the camera moves
TDTower switched from _physics_process to _process — eliminated physics-catchup bursts that spiked CPU on busy frames
Retarget timer staggered at build time; interval raised 0.15s → 0.25s; get_nodes_in_group replaced with TDEnemy.all_enemies static cache
Shader pre-warm at game start: all scene types + projectile tint materials compiled off-screen before wave 1 (was causing 300–480ms one-frame hitches)
TDBomb blast ring mesh/material shared (was allocating new StandardMaterial3D per explosion); TDProjectile changed from Area3D to Node3D (broadphase for nothing)
LOS raycast exclusions added (self + enemy); enemy look_at throttled to once per waypoint segment; flash timer replaces per-hit tweens

items.title: Display build version in game
items.note: version stored in project.godot as application/config/version (e.g. 2026.06.09.1); displayed bottom-right in small muted text via HUD VersionLabel

children.title: apply versioning system to game
display version in game

items.title: Early wave start: player can start the next wave 2s after the prior one begins
items.note: Replaced the alive-enemies gate with a 2s cooldown timer. _spawning no longer blocks the button — only the cooldown does.

items.title: Tower range preview when placing and selecting
items.note: Translucent sphere shown on slot hover (for chosen build type) and on selected tower. Implemented as part of the spherical range + LOS work.

items.title: Screenshot test map for visual checks
items.note: Added scenes/td_test_map.tscn — a flat straight spawn→goal lane with 8 tower pads (4 per side) and no walls — plus test/shot_test_map.gd, a headed --script helper that builds one of each tower type, spawns an enemy so turrets aim across the view, and saves a PNG. The main map's isometric camera + walls made offscreen screenshots fiddly (empty frames, occlusion); the test map gives a clean head-on stage. Reuses td_game.gd + the HUD, so HUD/economy checks work there too. Documented in VISUAL_CHECKLIST.md.

items.title: Tower visual improvements
items.note: Shipped: turrets now swing toward targets at a max yaw rate (projectile towers hold fire until aimed) instead of snapping; each type has a distinct head silhouette (Cannon box+barrel, Frost crystalline prism, Beam slim emitter cone, Bomb mortar dome+tube); damaged towers show a floating green/red health bar; floating damage numbers use Label3D.fixed_size so they stay constant on-screen size regardless of camera zoom.

children.title: towers don't immediately snap to the enemy, they must rotate slowly
towers should be clearly identifiable by shape and damage type
towers should show visible damage
floating damage values should be of absolute size, not scale with map zoom

items.title: Maze / free-placement TD twist
items.note: We chose classic fixed-path for the first prototype. If that proves fun, revisit letting players build the path with tower placement (Desktop TD / GemCraft style) for deeper strategy.

items.title: Perf: pool short-lived nodes (projectiles, blast rings)
items.note: Damage numbers are already pooled. Projectiles and blast rings are still allocated per-spawn. Not yet confirmed as a bottleneck — profile before investing.
